data.body:
MILESTONE: e4df CLOSED (change 660 Verified+1 + LLM-Review+1; completion-verifier signed). 649/650/660 all fully GREEN (flakes cleared on recheck). THREE children done: 368c, 42d1 (closed earlier), e4df (closed now).
664 material-half authoritative-input fix WORKED but PS6 surfaced a DEEPER, coupled finding (verify_opcert.py:204): the era check verifies key validity at the cert's SELF-CHOSEN merged_log_commit (only constrained to ancestor-of-HEAD) — a holder of a REVOKED/not-yet-added key can freshly sign a cert binding a PRE-REVOCATION commit where the key was valid, and it verifies. My material recompute doesn't stop this (attacker uses the real material). The 'ancestor of HEAD' constraint isn't enough — attacker picks WHICH ancestor.
This is the era-anchor DESIGN FORK, coupled to the escalated authoritative-state/key-rotation model: (A) era-at-HEAD (merge-gate requires the env key valid at the gated commit; rejects since-revoked envs — clean, self-contained, kills the attack, but changes era semantics: past-era certs by since-rotated keys fail); vs (B) era-at-cert with an INDEPENDENT authoritative anchor for the bound commit (needs produce-side design). Recommend A. NOT patching in isolation further — surfacing the fork to owner (it changes the security model + intersects 8f1d/ee0b key-rotation).
